info.title: CleanSheets Backend
info.description: Bridge between the CleanSheets iOS app, Google Sheets, Enable Banking, and Supabase. All endpoints require `Authorization: Bearer <google_id_token>` unless noted. Sign in with Google at the top of this page to auto-authorize Try-it-out requests.

googleIdToken.description: Google ID token issued for the CleanSheets client ID

id.description: Bank-assigned transaction id (Enable Banking entry_reference). Not unique across split transaction lines — dedup uses the composite key id+amount+date (see POST /api/sheet/save)

amount.description: Always positive — direction carries the sign

direction.description: debit = money out (spend), credit = money in (income/refund)

status.description: pending = reserved at the bank, not yet settled — shown instantly so the user recognizes the purchase; amount (and rarely id) can still change when it books

logTabMissing.description: true when the sheet has no _log tab — nothing was filtered out; the app should warn the user

amount.description: Amount to add to the Actual cell. Negative allowed so a refund can reduce the total.

date.description: The queue entry's `date` — part of the composite dedup key

status.description: The queue entry's `status` at save time — decides how the dedup key matches later (booked: id+amount+date, pending: id only)

name.description: New category name. Names the sheet scanners treat as structure (a "Total…" prefix, a section name) are rejected — such a row would be invisible to future saves.

budget.description: Optional Budget value for the new row (written to the section's Budget column)

transactionId.description: The queue entry's `id`. Provide together with `amount`, `date` and `status` to categorize the transaction to the new category in the same request.

amount.description: Amount written to the new row's Actual cell. Negative allowed so a refund can reduce the total.

date.description: The queue entry's `date` — part of the composite dedup key

status.description: The queue entry's `status` at save time — decides how the dedup key matches later (booked: id+amount+date, pending: id only)

row.description: 1-based sheet row of the new category

cell.description: Actual cell written — only when a transaction was categorized

newActual.description: Actual value after the write — only when a transaction was categorized

Unauthorized.description: Missing or invalid Google ID token

post.summary: Sign in — verify ID token and create the user row if missing

hasConfig.description: true when a sheet_id is saved

hasSheetsAccess.description: true when a Sheets-scope refresh token is stored

get.summary: Start the Google Sheets consent flow (action=start)
get.description: Returns the Google consent URL that grants the backend a Sheets-scope refresh token. Open the returned URL in a browser. The callback (`/auth/google/callback`) is browser-only and not testable here.

get.summary: Start the Enable Banking consent flow
get.description: Returns the bank authorization URL (MitID in production, instant approval at Mock ASPSP in sandbox). Open the returned URL in a browser. In sandbox `bank`/`country` default to the Mock ASPSP; in production both are required. The callback (`/auth/bank/callback`) is browser-only and not testable here.

parameters.name: bank
parameters.description: ASPSP name as listed by Enable Banking, e.g. "Nordea"

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Two-letter ISO country code of the bank

400.description: Missing/invalid bank selection or bank rejected the request

500.description: Server configuration, credential, or storage failure — message names the failing step

get.summary: Fetch uncategorized bank transactions, newest first
get.description: Fetches booked and pending (reserved) transactions from the last 30 days via the caller's stored Enable Banking session (all consented accounts), filters out entries already saved to the sheet's `_log` tab, and returns the rest newest first. The filter matches `_log` rows saved as booked on the composite key id+amount+date (banks reuse one id across split transaction lines), and rows saved as pending on id alone (a pending transaction can change amount/date when it books). Pending transactions are included so a purchase appears while the user still remembers it; they carry `status: "pending"`. Returns `BANK_TOKEN_EXPIRED` when the bank consent is missing, expired, or revoked — sessions cannot be refreshed, the user must reconnect. A missing `_log` tab is not an error: nothing is filtered and `logTabMissing` is true so the app can warn.

401.description: `GOOGLE_TOKEN_EXPIRED` (missing/invalid ID token) or `BANK_TOKEN_EXPIRED` (bank consent missing, expired, or revoked — reconnect)

404.description: No sheet configured yet, or the configured sheet is not accessible

500.description: Server configuration, credential, or storage failure — message names the failing step

post.summary: Categorize a transaction — add its amount to the category's Actual cell
post.description: Finds the current month tab (by English month name, e.g. "July"), locates the section+category row via the caller's stored column mapping (defaults F/H), and adds `amount` to the Actual cell. A `_log` row (transactionId, section, category, amount, date, status, savedAt) is written BEFORE the Actual cell — crash-safety per product spec §7b: if the Actual write fails, the log entry blocks a retry from double-counting. The hidden `_log` tab is created on first save. Duplicates are rejected with 409: booked saves match on id+amount+date (split lines with a shared id stay saveable), pending saves on id alone. The Left column is never written to.

200.description: Saved — new Actual value and the row written

404.description: `SHEET_NOT_FOUND` (no sheet configured, sheet/month tab not accessible) or `CATEGORY_NOT_FOUND` (no row for section+category)

500.description: `SHEET_WRITE_FAILED` (a write failed — if the `_log` row was already written, retrying returns 409 and the Actual cell was not changed) or `SUPABASE_ERROR`

delete.summary: Undo a save — reverse the Actual write and remove the _log entry
delete.description: Finds the newest `_log` row for `transactionId` (split lines share an id; undo follows immediately after a save), subtracts its logged amount from the section+category Actual cell of the current month tab, then deletes the `_log` row so the transaction returns to the queue. Operates only on the verified caller's own sheet (their stored config and Google consent).

200.description: Undone — new Actual value and the row reversed

404.description: No `_log` entry for this transactionId (`INVALID_REQUEST`), or sheet/month tab/category row not found (`SHEET_NOT_FOUND` / `CATEGORY_NOT_FOUND`)

409.description: The `_log` entry is incomplete (legacy row) and cannot be reversed (`INVALID_REQUEST`)

post.summary: Create a new category row — optionally categorize a transaction to it in one step
post.description: Creates a new category in the `section` box of the current month tab by writing `name` (and the optional `budget`) into the box's first empty row — the spare formatted rows above the section's Total. Nothing is inserted or shifted, so the dashboard layout, formatting, and Total formulas are untouched; the Left column is never written to. A duplicate category name in the section is rejected with 409, as is a box with no empty row left (add one in the sheet first). When `transactionId` + `amount` + `date` + `status` are provided, the transaction is saved to the new category in the same request with the exact `/api/sheet/save` semantics: the dedup gate runs BEFORE the row is written (an already-saved transaction leaves no orphan category), and the `_log` entry is written before the Actual cell (crash-safety per product spec §7b). If the request dies after the name was written, retrying returns 409 (duplicate category) — save the transaction via POST /api/sheet/save instead.

200.description: Created — the new row, plus the Actual cell and value when a transaction was categorized

400.description: Validation failed (`INVALID_REQUEST`) — bad body, save fields not provided together, name conflicts with sheet structure, or no Budget column mapped while `budget` was given

404.description: `SHEET_NOT_FOUND` (no sheet configured, sheet/month tab not accessible) or `CATEGORY_NOT_FOUND` (section box not found in the tab)

409.description: Category already exists in the section, the section has no empty row left, or the transaction was already saved (`INVALID_REQUEST`)

get.summary: List tabs, or scan one tab's sections/categories/columns
get.description: Without `tab`: returns the spreadsheet's tab names. With `tab`: scans the tab and returns detected sections, their categories (with Budget/Actual values), and column positions. Opens the sheet with the caller's stored Sheets consent — a sheet the account cannot access returns `SHEET_NOT_FOUND`; missing/expired consent returns `GOOGLE_TOKEN_EXPIRED`.

parameters.name: sheetId
parameters.description: Google Sheet ID (from the sheet URL)

parameters.name: tab
parameters.description: Tab name to scan, e.g. "July". Omit to list tabs.

200.description: Tab list (no `tab` param) or detected structure (`tab` given)

404.description: Sheet or tab not found, or not accessible to this Google account

200.description: Current config (fields are null until saved)

post.summary: Save sheet ID and/or column mapping
